citation: Schoenfeld BJ, Aragon AA, Wilborn C, Urbina SL, Hayward SE, Krieger J. 2017, PeerJ
intervention: RT 3x/wk (total-body, 3 sets 8–12 reps), maintenance calories; PRE group consumed 25 g protein + 1 g carb immediately pre-exercise vs POST group immediately post-exercise
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: FFM: PRE-SUPP +0.3 kg (64.5→64.8); POST-SUPP +0.4 kg (62.6→63.0); no significant group×time interaction (p>0.05). SD not reported in abstract; full text shows non-significant within-group changes only.
fat_change_kg: PRE-SUPP: −1.3 kg (12.2→10.9); POST-SUPP: −1.0 kg (8.9→7.9); significant time effect p=0.001, no group difference
weight_change_kg: PRE-SUPP: −1.6 kg; POST-SUPP: −0.7 kg (no significant group difference)
data_quality: DXA (dual x-ray absorptiometry); free full text PMC5214805; moderate confidence — small n, SD not reported for change scores in abstract

citation: Campbell BI, Aguilar D, Colenso-Semple LM, Hartke K, Fleming AR, Fox CD, Longstrom JM, Burkhart C, Conlin GA, Freidenreich D, Miller GD, Schoenfeld BJ. 2018, Int J Sport Nutr Exerc Metab
intervention: RT ~4x/wk (split routine), hypercaloric diet; HP: 2.5 g·kg⁻¹·d⁻¹ protein vs LP: 0.9 g·kg⁻¹·d⁻¹ protein. Aspiring physique athletes.
weight_change_kg: Not explicitly reported as change score; baseline ~61 kg; HP net ~+1.0 kg, LP net ~−0.1 kg (derived from FFM+FM)
data_quality: DXA confirmed; paywall (IJSNEM); moderate confidence — very small n=17, SD for change scores not reported (only pre/post absolute values); Schoenfeld is corresponding/last author

citation: Miller T, Mull S, Aragon AA, Krieger J, Schoenfeld BJ. 2018, Int J Sport Nutr Exerc Metab
n: 40 total (RT n=10; DIET n=10; RT+DIET n=10; CON n=10)
intervention: RT 3x/wk (progressive, all major muscle groups) ± dietary intervention (caloric deficit, protein 3.1 g·kg⁻¹·d⁻¹ for diet groups) in a 2×2 factorial design; premenopausal women
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: Only RT group showed significant lean mass increase (qualitative; abstract states 'only RT group showed significant increases in lean mass'). Specific kg ± SD not extractable from public sources — full text paywalled.
fat_change_kg: All 3 experimental groups decreased fat mass significantly; slope steepest in RT+DIET group (qualitative from abstract/PubMed record). Specific kg ± SD not publicly available.
data_quality: DXA (enCORE 2012 software v14.1); full text paywalled; confidence LOW for specific numbers — qualitative direction confirmed, quantitative kg values not independently verified

citation: Vargas-Molina S, Petro JL, Romance R, Kreider RB, Schoenfeld BJ, Bonilla DA, Benítez-Porres J. 2020, J Int Soc Sports Nutr
intervention: RT 4x/wk (upper/lower split, nonlinear periodization); KD: ~30–40 g/d carbs, 1.7 g·kg⁻¹·d⁻¹ protein, ~40 kcal·kg-FFM⁻¹·d⁻¹ vs NKD: ~60% carbs, same protein, ~45 kcal·kg-FFM⁻¹·d⁻¹
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: FFM — KD: −0.7±1.7 kg; NKD: +0.7±1.1 kg (between-group difference significant)
data_quality: DXA confirmed; free full text PMC7146906; moderate confidence — small n=21; NKD group was slight caloric surplus (~45 kcal/kg-FFM) which explains FFM gain

citation: Ribeiro AS, Pereira LC, Schoenfeld BJ, Nunes JP, Kassiano W, Nabuco HCG, Sugihara Junior P, Fernandes RR, Antunes M, Cyrino ES. 2022, Med Sci Sports Exerc
intervention: RT 3x/wk (8 exercises, 3 sets, 8–15 reps); LP: low protein intake; MP: moderate protein; HP: higher protein. Older women (mean age 68.7±5.6 yr).
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: Skeletal muscle mass (SMM) % change only publicly reported — LP: +2.3%; MP: +5.4%; HP: +5.1%. Absolute kg with SD not available in abstract or public summary.
fat_change_kg: % change only — LP: −1.7%; MP: −3.7%; HP: −3.1%. Absolute kg with SD not publicly available.
data_quality: DXA confirmed (whole-body); full text paywalled (Med Sci Sports Exerc); confidence LOW for specific values — percentage change only, no kg ± SD independently verifiable from public access

citation: Plotkin DL, Coleman M, Van Every DW, Maldonado J, Oberlin DJ, Israetel M, Feather J, Alto A, Vigotsky AD, Schoenfeld BJ. 2022, PeerJ
intervention: RT 2x/wk (4 sets × 4 lower-body exercises); LOAD group progressively increased weight (constant 8–12 rep range) vs REPS group increased reps (constant load). Resistance-trained (≥1 yr lower-body training).
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: Leg segmental lean mass (BIA) — LOAD: 16.5±3.3→16.8±3.3 kg (Δ+0.3±0.4 kg); REPS: 17.0±4.0→17.3±3.9 kg (Δ+0.3±0.3 kg); adjusted between-group difference 0.1 [−0.1 to 0.3] kg
weight_change_kg: Not separately reported; derived change modest across both groups
data_quality: BIA (InBody 770 segmental) — NOT DXA; free full text PMC9528903; confidence MODERATE for lean mass direction, LOW for absolute FM change (BIA, no whole-body FM in kg)

citation: Morton RW, Murphy KT, McKellar SR, Schoenfeld BJ, Henselmans M, Helms E, Aragon AA, Devries MC, Banfield L, Krieger JW, Phillips SM. 2018, Br J Sports Med
sex: Mixed (separate subgroup analyses by sex not reported for FFM primary outcome)
training_status: Mixed trained + untrained; subgroup reported separately
intervention: Protein supplementation (avg +35 g/d above habitual ~1.4 g/kg/d) during RT; no single protocol — meta-regression across 49 RCTs
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: FFM: protein +2.24 ± SE vs control +1.94 ± SE; difference +0.30 kg (95% CI: 0.09–0.52, p=0.007). Trained subgroup: difference +0.75 kg (95% CI: 0.09–1.40, p=0.03). Untrained subgroup: difference +0.15 kg (95% CI: −0.02–0.31, p=0.08)
fat_change_kg: Not primary outcome; not meta-analyzed
data_quality: Pooled meta-analysis of 49 RCTs; body composition measured by DXA, hydrodensitometry, or BodPod across included trials; free full text via PMC (PMC5867436); HIGH confidence for pooled estimate, moderate for subgroup

citation: Morton RW, Oikawa SY, Wavell CG, Mazara N, McGlory C, Quadrilatero J, Baechler BL, Baker SK, Phillips SM. 2016, J Appl Physiol
intervention: RT 4x/week (HR: 3×20–25 reps at 30–50% 1RM to failure vs LR: 3×8–12 reps at 75–90% 1RM to failure) + 30 g whey protein twice daily (post-exercise and before sleep). No placebo control — both groups received whey.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: FFM (whole body FBFM, DXA, both groups combined): +1.2 kg (64.6 ± 1.1 → 65.8 ± 1.1 kg, p<0.01); no significant difference between HR and LR groups. Appendicular lean mass: 33.1 ± 0.6 → 34.0 ± 0.6 kg (+0.9 kg, p<0.001). Leg lean mass: 24.4 ± 0.5 → 25.0 ± 0.5 kg (+0.6 kg, p<0.001)
fat_change_kg: Not reported as a primary outcome (fat mass baseline: HR 19.4 ± 2 kg, LR 16.9 ± 1 kg; post values not clearly tabulated)
data_quality: DXA + muscle biopsy (fiber CSA); RCT but no protein-dose placebo control (both groups got whey); free full text PMC4967245; MODERATE confidence — confounds whey dose effect with load effect

citation: Longland TM, Oikawa SY, Mitchell CJ, Devries MC, Phillips SM. 2016, Am J Clin Nutr
training_status: Untrained (no formal RT background specified; studied during boot-camp-style intensive exercise)
intervention: ~40% energy deficit + RT 6x/week (resistance + HIIT combined). PRO: 2.4 g protein/kg/d; CON: 1.2 g protein/kg/d
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: Lean body mass (4-compartment model): PRO +1.2 ± 1.0 kg; CON +0.1 ± 1.0 kg (PRO > CON, p<0.05)
weight_change_kg: PRO: ~−3.6 kg estimated; CON: ~−3.4 kg estimated (calculated from LBM + fat changes, net negative both groups)
data_quality: 4-compartment model (gold-standard body composition); RCT; paywalled at AJCN — abstract data confirmed via PubMed; HIGH confidence for reported values. NOTE: 4-week duration + extreme energy deficit + HIIT limits generalizability to normal muscle-gain context

citation: Hartman JW, Tang JE, Wilkinson SB, Tarnopolsky MA, Lawrence RL, Fullerton AV, Phillips SM. 2007, Am J Clin Nutr
n: 56 (Milk n=18, Soy n=19, CHO control n=19)
intervention: RT 5x/week (rotating split-body program) + post-exercise beverage immediately and 1h after: (A) fat-free fluid milk, (B) isonitrogenous/isoenergetic soy protein, (C) isoenergetic maltodextrin (control)
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: DXA fat- and bone-free mass increased in all groups; Milk > Soy and Milk > CHO (p<0.05); Soy vs CHO not significantly different. Specific kg ± SD values not available in abstract — full text behind paywall (AJCN)
fat_change_kg: Not reported in abstract
data_quality: DXA + muscle biopsy fiber CSA; 3-arm RCT; McMaster group; paywalled (AJCN 2007); abstract-level data only — actual mean ± SD kg changes CANNOT be confirmed without full text access. MODERATE confidence for direction of effect, LOW for precise kg values

citation: Bagheri R, Hooshmand Moghadam B, Jo E, Tinsley GM, Stratton MT, Church DD, Eskandari M, Moghadam BH, Dutheil F, Wong A. 2024, Front Nutr
n: 48 total (4 groups × 12: CT+1.6, CT+3.2, RT+1.6, RT+3.2)
intervention: RT 4x/week (periodized 75–90% 1RM, upper/lower split) vs concurrent training (RT + cycling HIIT), crossed with protein intake of 1.6 or 3.2 g/kg/d (40g whey post-exercise + dietary protein)
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: Upper body lean mass (DXA): CT+1.6: +1.15 kg, CT+3.2: +1.26 kg, RT+1.6: +1.20 kg, RT+3.2: +1.00 kg. Lower body lean mass: CT+1.6: +0.68 kg, CT+3.2: +0.66 kg, RT+1.6: +0.82 kg, RT+3.2: +1.00 kg. No significant between-group differences in lean mass gains
fat_change_kg: Not reported with mean ± SD by group
data_quality: DXA; RCT 2×2 factorial design; free full text PMC11349518; NOTE: this is NOT a Phillips/McMaster study — included because it directly tests 1.6 vs 3.2 g/kg in trained males with DXA; MODERATE-HIGH confidence

citation: Bell KE, Snijders T, Zulyniak M, Kumbhare D, Parise G, Chabowski A, Phillips SM. 2017, PLoS One
intervention: Phase 1 (6 wk): supplement only. Phase 2 (12 wk): supplement + RT (2x/week RT + 1x/week HIIT). SUPP: whey protein 30g + creatine 2.5g + vit D 500IU + calcium + n-3 PUFA twice daily. CON: 22g maltodextrin twice daily
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: Phase 1 (supplementation only, 6 wk): SUPP +1.2 ± 0.3 kg; CON −0.1 ± 0.2 kg (p<0.001). Full 20-week total lean mass change (combined phases) not separately reported in abstract with SD
fat_change_kg: Not reported in abstract with SD
data_quality: DXA; double-blind RCT; free full text PubMed (PMID 28719669); CAUTION: multi-ingredient supplement (whey + creatine + vit D + n-3 PUFA) — cannot attribute lean mass gain solely to protein; MODERATE confidence for protein-specific effect

citation: Garthe I, Raastad T, Refsnes PE, Sundgot-Borgen J. 2013, Eur J Sport Sci
intervention: RT 4x/wk (sport-specific + strength) during 8–12 wk weight-gain period; NCG received individualized dietary counseling targeting ~500 kcal/day surplus (~3,585 kcal/day); ALG ate ad libitum (~2,964 kcal/day). High protein both groups.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: NCG: +1.7 kg LBM; ALG: +1.2 kg LBM (between-group difference NS; SDs not reported in abstract — full-text values cited via Iraki 2019 review)
fat_change_kg: NCG: +1.1 kg; ALG: +0.2 kg (abstract reports FM increased 15±4% vs 3±3%)
weight_change_kg: NCG: +3.9% BW; ALG: +1.5% BW (from ~70–75 kg baseline, approx +2.8 kg vs +1.1 kg)
data_quality: DXA (MeSH: Absorptiometry, Photon confirmed). Free full text: No (paywalled, Wiley). Confidence: MODERATE — absolute kg LBM values (1.7 / 1.2) sourced from Iraki 2019 narrative review citing full-text table; abstract gives % FM only; SD on LBM changes not retrievable without full text access.

citation: Ribeiro AS, Nunes JP, Schoenfeld BJ, et al. 2019, J Human Kinet (J Hum Kinet)
intervention: RT 6 days/wk (competitive bodybuilders, off-season). G1: 67.5±1.7 kcal/kg/day (~6,088 kcal/day; ~4,063 kcal above RMR). G2: 50.1±0.5 kcal/kg/day (~4,501 kcal/day; ~2,512 kcal above RMR). Both high-protein. Massive hypercaloric surplus.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: G1: +1.0 kg skeletal muscle mass (36.7±3.7 → 37.7±3.9 kg); G2: +0.4 kg (36.1±1.1 → 36.5±1.2 kg). Note: 'skeletal muscle mass' estimated via Lee et al. prediction equation, not direct DXA FFM.
fat_change_kg: G1: +1.2 kg fat mass (body fat 16.2±4.6% → 17.4±4.6% at baseline BW ~90 kg, approx +1.2 kg); G2: +0.1 kg (13.3±2.7% → 13.4±2.6%). SD on fat mass change not directly reported.
weight_change_kg: Not explicitly reported as absolute change; baseline G1 ~90.2±13.3 kg, G2 ~89.8±3.8 kg
data_quality: 7-site skinfold (Lange caliper, Jackson & Pollock 1978 equation) + Lee et al. skeletal muscle mass prediction. No DXA. Free full text: Yes (PMC). Confidence: LOW-MODERATE — skinfold only, pilot study (n=11), only 4 weeks, massive surplus not typical of 'lean bulk'. Fat mass in kg back-calculated from % BF × BW approximation.

citation: Antonio J, Peacock CA, Ellerbroek A, Fromhoff B, Silver T. 2014, J Int Soc Sports Nutr
intervention: Self-directed RT (8.9±6.7 yrs training experience). HP group: 4.4 g/kg/day protein, ~2,835 kcal/day (~793 kcal surplus vs baseline). Control: ~2,052 kcal/day (below baseline). Both groups maintained normal training.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: Control: +1.3±2.0 kg FFM; HP: +1.9±2.4 kg FFM (no significant between-group difference)
fat_change_kg: Control: +0.3±4.7 kg; HP: −0.2±2.2 kg (no significant difference)
data_quality: Air displacement plethysmography (Bod Pod). Free full text: Yes (PMC). Confidence: MODERATE — BodPod is a validated 2-compartment method, weaker than DXA for tracking small changes; uncontrolled training; HP group had more training experience (4.9 vs 2.4 yrs) — potential confounder. Large SD on fat change in control group (±4.7 kg) suggests measurement noise or compliance issues.

citation: Antonio J, Ellerbroek A, Silver T, et al. 2015, J Int Soc Sports Nutr
intervention: Periodized RT program. NP: 2.3 g/kg/day protein, ~2,119 kcal/day (near baseline). HP: 3.4 g/kg/day protein, ~2,614 kcal/day (~495 kcal above NP/baseline). HP group had more training experience (4.9±4.1 vs 2.4±1.7 yrs).
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: NP: +1.5±1.8 kg FFM; HP: +1.5±2.2 kg FFM (no significant difference between groups)
fat_change_kg: NP: −0.3±2.2 kg; HP: −1.7±2.3 kg (HP significantly greater fat loss, p<0.05)
data_quality: Air displacement plethysmography (Bod Pod). Free full text: Yes (PMC). Confidence: MODERATE — BodPod 2-compartment, uncontrolled free-living training, HP group had more experience (confounder), high calorie surplus attributable to protein not independent energy surplus per se.

citation: Haun CT, Vann CG, Mobley CB, et al. 2018, Front Nutr
intervention: Extreme-volume RT (10 sets/exercise week 1 escalating to 32 sets/exercise week 6), 3 days/wk. All groups targeted ~500 kcal/day surplus above TDEE. Protein manipulation: WP 25 g/day, GWP graded 25–150 g/day weeks 1–6, MALTO 30 g/day maltodextrin. Minimum squat 1RM ≥1.5× BW for inclusion.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: GWP: +2.93 kg LBM (DXA); MALTO: +2.35 kg LBM (DXA). WP: significant within-group increase (exact kg not isolated in available text). No significant between-group differences reported.
fat_change_kg: GWP: −1.00 kg (DXA, p<0.05); WP: significant decrease (p<0.05); MALTO: no significant change. Exact kg for WP and between-group SDs not available in accessible full text.
weight_change_kg: Not reported as group-level mean ± SD in available text
data_quality: DXA (ICC 0.998 same-day reliability reported). Free full text: Yes (PMC). Confidence: MODERATE-HIGH — DXA used, well-controlled surplus, trained men with rigorous inclusion criteria. Limitation: extreme RT volume is confounded with protein dose manipulation; exact mean ± SD for all three groups not fully recoverable from abstract/open text.

citation: Helms ER, Spence AJ, Sousa C, et al. 2023, Sports Med Open
n: 17 completers (originally 21; COVID-19 attrition). 3 groups: MAIN (maintenance), MOD (~5% surplus), HIGH (~15% surplus)
intervention: Supervised RT 3x/wk. MAIN: estimated energy maintenance. MOD: ~5% above TDEE. HIGH: ~15% above TDEE. Protein equated across groups. Primary outcomes: skinfold thickness, muscle thickness (ultrasound), 1RM strength.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: Not reported as absolute kg — muscle thickness (ultrasound biceps brachii, triceps brachii, quadriceps) used as proxy for hypertrophy. Regression showed body mass change predicted skinfold change (R²=0.49) but did NOT predict muscle thickness or 1RM. No between-group differences in muscle thickness or strength.
fat_change_kg: Not reported as absolute kg fat mass — outcome was sum of skinfold thicknesses. Faster body mass gain (HIGH > MOD > MAIN) primarily predicted greater skinfold (fat) increase, not greater muscle thickness.
weight_change_kg: Body mass change was the key predictor variable; exact per-group mean ± SD not available from accessible abstract text
data_quality: Skinfold calipers (sum of skinfolds) + B-mode ultrasound (muscle thickness). No DXA. Free full text: Yes (PMC10620361). Confidence: MODERATE — n=17 completers is underpowered; skinfold less precise than DXA; ultrasound muscle thickness is valid hypertrophy proxy but not FFM in kg. Key finding (more surplus → more fat not more muscle) is directionally robust across the regression even without group-level kg data.

citation: Longland et al. 2016, Am J Clin Nutr
training_status: untrained (recreationally active 1-2x/wk, no structured RT prior)
intervention: RT + HIIT 6 d/wk; 40% energy deficit; high-protein (2.4 g/kg/d) vs low-protein control (1.2 g/kg/d); overweight men (BMI >25, mean ~97 kg)
data_quality: 4-compartment model (gold standard); full text free via AJCN; high confidence. Note: short duration, severe deficit, HIIT+RT combo — extreme conditions. LP group gained trivial LBM but recomp threshold met by HP only (significant p<0.05).

citation: Antonio et al. 2015, J Int Soc Sports Nutr
training_status: recreational/trained (mean RT experience HP 4.9 ± 4.1 y, NP 2.4 ± 1.7 y; unequal baseline p<0.05)
intervention: RT upper-lower split 5 d/wk; high-protein (HP 3.4 g/kg/d, ~2614 kcal) vs normal protein (NP 2.3 g/kg/d, ~2119 kcal); hypercaloric surplus in HP
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: FFM (BodPod): HP +1.5 ± 2.2 kg; NP +1.5 ± 1.8 kg
fat_change_kg: HP −1.7 ± 2.3 kg; NP −0.3 ± 2.2 kg (HP significantly greater p<0.05)
data_quality: BodPod (2-compartment, validated test-retest); free full text PMC4617900; moderate-high confidence. Large SD relative to mean change — notable interindividual variability. Unequal group sizes and baseline training experience difference are limitations.

citation: Campbell et al. 2018, Int J Sport Nutr Exerc Metab
training_status: recreational/trained (aspiring physique athletes, at least 3 mo RT, deadlift ~1.4-1.6× BM)
intervention: RT upper-lower split 4 d/wk; high-protein (HP 2.5 g/kg/d, ~1839 kcal) vs low-protein (LP 0.9 g/kg/d, ~1416 kcal)
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: FFM (A-mode ultrasound BodyMetrix BX-2000): HP +2.1 kg (47.1±4.5 → 49.2±5.4); LP +0.6 kg (48.1±2.7 → 48.7±2.0); group×time p=0.009
data_quality: A-mode ultrasound (2-compartment, inferior to DXA — validated against DXA but with higher technician variability); free preprint PDF available; moderate confidence. Small n per group (8-9). Change score SDs not reported in Table 2 — only pre/post values with SDs.

citation: Cribb et al. 2006, Int J Sport Nutr Exerc Metab
training_status: recreational (recreational bodybuilders, ≥2 y RT, 1RM squat and bench ~1.0× BM — modest strength levels)
intervention: RT progressive linear overload 3-phase (70→80→90% 1RM); whey isolate (WI) vs casein (C) supplementation 1.5 g/kg/d added to habitual diet; total ~2.1 g protein/kg/d both groups
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: FFM (DXA): WI +5.0 ± 0.3 kg (significant); C +0.8 ± 0.4 kg (significant but smaller p<0.01)
fat_change_kg: WI −1.5 ± 0.5 kg (p<0.05); C +0.2 ± 0.3 kg (no recomp in casein group)
data_quality: DXA (3-compartment); abstract on PubMed free, full text paywalled at Human Kinetics; moderate confidence. Very small n (13 total). SD values for change are unusually tight (±0.3–0.5 kg) suggesting possible SE rather than SD — flag for verification. WI group clear recomp; casein group is control comparator.

citation: Haun et al. 2018, Front Nutr
training_status: trained (college-aged males, training age 5 ± 3 y, back squat 1RM ≥1.5× BM required for inclusion)
intervention: RT full-body 3 d/wk, extreme volume progression (10→32 sets/exercise/wk at 60% 1RM); 3 arms: graded whey protein (GWP, 25→150 g/d), whey protein (WP, 25 g/d), maltodextrin (MALTO, 30 g/d); all groups ~500 kcal surplus, ~2.2-2.3 g protein/kg/d self-reported
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: LBM (DXA, fat- and bone-free mass): GWP +2.93 kg (p<0.05); MALTO +2.35 kg (p<0.05); WP change not reported as statistically significant separately
fat_change_kg: GWP −1.00 kg (p<0.05); MALTO not significant; WP not significant (Barakat review Table 3 reports WP FM −0.65 kg)
data_quality: DXA (3-compartment); free full text PMC6141782; moderate confidence. SDs for change values not clearly reported in PMC full text (only mean changes and p-values in main table); supplementary data may contain SDs. Unusual finding: recomp in hypercaloric surplus with extreme volume progression in already-trained men.

citation: Rauch et al. 2018, Sports (Basel)
training_status: trained (NCAA Division II collegiate volleyball players, ≥1 y RT, 1RM squat:BM ratio ~1.1; off-season after detraining period; baseline BF ~29%)
intervention: RT full-body 3 d/wk power-oriented (squat, bench, deadlift); OTL (optimum power velocity) vs PVBT (progressive velocity-based); all received 25 g whey post-workout; all consumed ~1.6 g protein/kg/d; sport-specific conditioning continued
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: LBM (DXA): OTL +2.7 kg (CI: 1.5–4.3 kg, ~5.4% increase); PVBT +2.7 kg (CI: 1.4–3.8 kg, ~5.3% increase); no between-group difference
fat_change_kg: OTL −2.7 kg (CI: −3.7 to −0.9 kg, ~8.5% decrease); PVBT −2.1 kg (CI: −3.1 to −0.6 kg, ~10.1% decrease); no between-group difference
data_quality: DXA (3-compartment); free full text PMC6316804; moderate confidence. Very small n per group (7-8). Change values reported as CI not SD — precise SDs not extractable from PMC text. Higher baseline BF (~29%) likely facilitated the large FM loss. Detraining + return to training context amplifies recomp signal.

citation: Kuehne et al. 2024, BMC Sports Science Medicine Rehabilitation
intervention: RT 2x/wk, 4×4 reps ≥85% 1RM (half squat or trap-bar deadlift) + accessory work; no dietary prescription
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: +0.84 ± 1.12 kg total lean mass (combined groups); squat +0.9 ± 1.2, deadlift +0.8 ± 1.1
fat_change_kg: not reported as kg; body fat% changes: squat −0.3 ± 3.9%, deadlift −0.8 ± 3.2%
data_quality: DXA; free full text PMC; moderate confidence — small n=22, only 8 weeks, SD large relative to mean

citation: Sherwood et al. 2025, Nutrients
intervention: RT 2x/wk upper-body (3×10 reps @75% 1RM wks 1–8, 4×8 @80% wks 9–12) + HIIT cycling 3x/wk; CON vs whey PRO supplement; eucaloric (no prescribed deficit)
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: CON +0.84 ± 0.80 kg lean mass; PRO +0.56 ± 1.40 kg lean mass (DXA total lean)
data_quality: DXA; free full text PMC; moderate confidence — small n per group (12/15), combined RT+HIIT confounds pure RT effect

citation: Thalacker-Mercer et al. 2010, Obesity (Silver Spring)
training_status: untrained (postmenopausal, no RT in prior 12 months; overweight BMI ~29)
intervention: RT 3x/wk, 3 sets 8–12 reps @80% 1RM (bilateral leg extension/curl/press, chest press, lat pull); 500 kcal/day deficit (hypoenergetic diet imposed on all)
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: RT group: FFM −0.3 ± 0.4 kg (vs sedentary −1.6 ± 0.4 kg; group difference p≤0.05) — RT preserved FFM under deficit
fat_change_kg: RT group: −4.7 ± 0.5 kg; sedentary −4.1 ± 0.9 kg
data_quality: 3-compartment model (air-displacement plethysmography + D2O dilution); free full text PMC; high methodological confidence but confounded by 500 kcal/day deficit — not a muscle-gain study; useful only as deficit-RT FFM-preservation datum

citation: Volek et al. 2010, Nutrition & Metabolism
n: 16 women (LC+RT n=8, RT n=8; 18 randomized, 2 dropped)
training_status: untrained (no regular RT for ≥6 months; age 20–40 yr)
intervention: RT-only group: 2x/wk full-body RT (12 RM wks 1–5, 8 RM wks 6–10), self-selected eucaloric diet (~41% CHO, 34% fat, 17% protein). Ketogenic group (separate arm) also reported for context.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: RT-only (Ex) group: +1.6 ± 1.8 kg lean body mass
fat_change_kg: RT-only (Ex) group: −0.6 ± 0.8 kg
data_quality: DXA; free full text PMC; moderate confidence — small n=8 in RT arm, 10 weeks only, within-group significance p=0.05 (borderline); no separate control arm (only LC vs normal diet comparison)

citation: Timbergen et al. 2021, Frontiers in Physiology
n: 31 early postmenopausal women (ET n=15, placebo n=16)
training_status: untrained (early postmenopausal, no prior structured RT)
intervention: RT 3x/wk supervised, 6 exercises (leg press, knee ext/flex, triceps, sit-ups, back ext), progressive loading; estrogen patch vs placebo (double-blind RCT)
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: ET group: +2.2 kg FFM (41.3→43.5 kg, +5.5%); Placebo: +1.3 kg FFM (41.6→42.9 kg, +2.9%); both p<0.05; SD not reported for change (only baseline SDs: ±4.4 and ±5.5 kg respectively)
fat_change_kg: Not reported in published tables
data_quality: DXA (whole-body FFM) + MRI (quad CSA); free full text PMC; high confidence for FFM direction but SD of change not reported — calculated from pre/post means only; estrogen intervention confounds generalizability; placebo arm (+1.3 kg FFM) is most useful for unmedicated reference

citation: Josse et al. 2011, Journal of Nutrition
n: 90 randomized, 81 completers (HPHD n=30, APMD n=30, APLD n=30)
intervention: Aerobic 5x/wk @~250 kcal/session + RT 2x/wk (upper/lower split); ~500 kcal/day deficit; three protein/dairy levels (HPHD ~30% pro, APLD ~15% pro)
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: HPHD: +0.7 ± 0.3 kg lean mass; APMD: −0.2 ± 0.2 kg; APLD: −0.7 ± 0.3 kg (all from DXA; values mean ± SE not SD)
fat_change_kg: All groups significant fat loss; HPHD lost more trunk fat than APLD (p<0.005); pooled weight loss −4.3 ± 0.7 kg
data_quality: DXA (Hologic QDR-4500A); free full text PMC; high confidence methodologically, but heavily confounded by 500 kcal deficit + aerobic + RT combined — not isolatable RT effect. Lean mass values in SE not SD. Useful as recomp-under-deficit datum.

citation: Garthe et al. 2011, Int J Sport Nutr Exerc Metab
n: SR n=13, FR n=11 (of 24 randomized; 30 completed including 6-subject subgroup excluded from main analysis)
sex: Mixed (43% M / 57% F in SR; 31% M / 69% F in FR)
training_status: Elite athletes (Norwegian Olympic-level; 4 RT sessions/week during trial)
intervention: SR: ~19% energy intake reduction (~−469 kcal/d; protein 1.6 g/kg BW); FR: ~30% reduction (~−791 kcal/d; protein 1.4 g/kg BW). Both groups: 4 RT sessions/wk added to usual training. Target: 0.7%/wk (SR) vs 1.4%/wk (FR) BW loss.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: SR: +1.0 ± 0.2 kg LBM (±SE); FR: −0.3 ± 0.4 kg LBM (±SE). % change: SR +2.1% ± 0.4%, FR −0.2% ± 0.7%; between-group p<0.01.
data_quality: DEXA (Lunar DPX). Full text obtained (open PDF). Change column is ±SE not ±SD — limits variance interpretation. Duration differed between groups (SR 8.5±2.2 wk vs FR 5.3±0.9 wk). High confidence.

citation: Longland et al. 2016, Am J Clin Nutr
training_status: Recreationally active but untrained for RT (BMI >25, overweight young men; no prior structured RT)
intervention: Both groups: ~40% energy deficit (33 ± 1 kcal/kg LBM/d), 6 d/wk RT + HIIT. PRO: 2.4 g/kg/d protein; CON: 1.2 g/kg/d protein. All food provided.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: PRO: +1.2 ± 1.0 kg LBM; CON: +0.1 ± 1.0 kg LBM (between-group p<0.05)
fat_change_kg: PRO: −4.8 ± 1.6 kg; CON: −3.5 ± 1.4 kg (between-group p<0.05)
weight_change_kg: PRO: ~−3.6 kg (calculated: +1.2 FM change + −4.8 FM = net ~−3.6); CON: ~−3.4 kg. Not directly stated in abstract.
data_quality: 4-compartment model (gold standard for body composition). Free full text obtained (open PDF). ±SD for changes. Severe deficit (40%) + very high exercise volume (6d/wk) limits generalizability. High confidence for the numbers as reported.

citation: Mettler et al. 2010, Med Sci Sports Exerc
sex: Not specified in abstract (study at Univ. Birmingham; likely predominantly male resistance-trained athletes)
intervention: Both groups: 40% energy restriction (60% of habitual intake) for 2 weeks. HP: ~2.3 g/kg/d protein (35% energy); CP: ~1.0 g/kg/d (15% energy). Subjects continued their usual training.
fat_change_kg: Not significantly different between groups (stated in abstract: 'fat loss were not influenced by the diet'). Specific kg values not in abstract; full text reports fat mass changes were small and non-significant between groups.
data_quality: DXA (confirmed in search results citing MSSE source). Abstract only — fat mass kg values not extractable without full text. SE vs SD ambiguity not confirmed. Moderate-high confidence on LBM numbers (abstract clearly states ±SD). Very short duration (2 weeks) limits applicability to longer prep phases.

citation: Josse et al. 2011, J Nutr
training_status: Untrained (sedentary to light activity at baseline)
intervention: All groups: ~500 kcal/d deficit; aerobic 5 d/wk (250 kcal/session) + progressive RT 2 d/wk. HPHD: ~1.33 g/kg/d protein (30% energy, high dairy); APMD: ~0.84 g/kg/d (15% energy, medium dairy); APLD: ~0.72 g/kg/d (15% energy, low dairy).
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: HPHD: +0.7 ± 0.3 kg lean; APMD: −0.2 ± 0.2 kg; APLD: −0.7 ± 0.3 kg (HPHD vs APLD p<0.05, 8–16 wk). Values from PMC full text.
fat_change_kg: HPHD: ~−5.3 kg; APMD: ~−4.0 kg; APLD: ~−3.5 kg (estimated from Figure 1A in full text; ±SD not extractable from figure).
weight_change_kg: Pooled mean: −4.3 ± 0.7 kg across all groups. Group-specific values not separately stated.
data_quality: DXA (measurements at 0, 8, 16 wk). Free full text via PMC (PMC3159052). Lean mass ±SD extractable; fat mass values are figure-based estimates (moderate confidence on fat numbers). Overweight women, not trained athletes — limits applicability to lean/trained populations. Moderate-high confidence.

citation: Hulmi et al. 2017, Front Physiol (published Dec 2016 as vol 7:689)
training_status: Trained/advanced (competitive physique athletes, RT 4.7±0.7 d/wk at baseline)
intervention: COMP: self-directed contest prep diet (~22.9% caloric reduction vs. baseline); maintained RT ~4.7 d/wk + increased aerobic from 3.6 to 4.9 d/wk. Protein: 3.16 g/kg/d at baseline, 3.14 g/kg/d during diet. CTRL: no intervention.
lean_or_ffm_change_kg: COMP: DXA FFM unchanged (1.1 ± 3.1% change; ~+0.6 ± 1.6 kg estimated from baseline FFM ~55 kg). CTRL: no change. Note: bioimpedance showed small FFM decrease; DXA FFM was statistically unaltered.
fat_change_kg: COMP: −7.5 ± 2.6 kg (DXA; pre to peak diet/competition). CTRL: no change.
weight_change_kg: COMP: −7.8 kg [−11.9% of BW] pre to competition.
data_quality: DXA (primary) + MFBIA + skinfolds. Free full text (Frontiers open access). Observational/prospective cohort — not an RCT; no dietary control. Caloric deficit estimated, not precisely measured. Heterogeneous sports (fitness, figure, bikini). Long duration (~20 wk) adds ecological validity. Moderate confidence (no randomization, self-reported diet).
